<think> Jayne Mansfield was an American actress who was active in the 1950s and 1960s. "The House of Love" is a play that Mansfield starred in, and it is known to be a semi-autobiographical work. Given the time period of Mansfield's career, I would expect the play to have taken place during a time when Mansfield was active. The play is also known to be a semi-autobiographical work, which suggests that it may have been written and performed during a time when Mansfield was experiencing a significant event or period in her life. Considering Mansfield's personal life, I would expect the play to have taken place during the 1950s, when she was married to Paul Mansfield and had young children. The play's title, "The House of Love", also suggests a domestic setting, which is consistent with Mansfield's life during this time period. Therefore, I would expect the play to have taken place between 1950 and 1960.</think>
<answer> 1950-1960 </answer>
